title: 1. Getting started with idiographic
text: package ships self-regulated-learning (SRL) experience-sampling data Chapter 20 Learning Analytics Methods book ready--model dataset. Loading needs ordering, indexing, column selection — rows already ordered name day, day within-person occasion index can hand straight build_usem() build_gimme(). Thirty-six students contributed 156 occasions nine SRL indicators: Throughout method vignettes use focused set five indicators printed networks stay readable; nine available. chapter uses Grace single-person examples, keep convention. method needs single series, pass subject = "Grace" estimator rather slicing data frame .

what: Network Estimation from Intensive Longitudinal Data
title: Network Estimation from Intensive Longitudinal Data
text: Network estimation intensive longitudinal data — person-specific within-person temporal, contemporaneous, -subject networks ESM / EMA / diary panels, one tidy verb per method. idiographic estimates dynamic networks intensive longitudinal data (ILD): ordinary regularized vector autoregression, multilevel VAR, native Bayesian multilevel VAR matches Mplus DSEM, unified SEM, GIMME — plus supporting workflow (preprocessing audits, edge-stability diagnostics, rolling windows, forecast validation, model comparison). Every result tidy object access handful verbs: edges(), nodes(), coefs(), matrices(), summary(), plot().

title: Network Estimation from Intensive Longitudinal Data
text: Every estimator clean-room reimplementation, written published algorithms rather wrapping another package — validated numerical equivalence reference: algorithms native, package’s runtime footprint minimal — Imports: stats, utils, lme4, lavaan . mlVAR, graphicalVAR, glasso, MplusAutomation Suggests used solely regenerate validation fixtures; needed run estimator. Bayesian DSEM sampler particular highlight: build_mlvar_bayes() reproduces output mlVAR::mlVAR(estimator = "Mplus") — Mplus’s two-level Bayesian VAR latent mean centering — without Mplus installed, using pure-R conjugate Gibbs sampler hand-rolled inverse-Wishart draws (MCMCpack/rstan). validated real Mplus 9 output, independent Stan/JAGS implementation (Li et al., 2022), real openESM datasets, published network findings Bringmann et al. (2013).

title: Network Estimation from Intensive Longitudinal Data
text: Everything tidy verb named arguments returning tidy data.frame — never index result object reach sub-network coefficient.
code: library(idiographic)  ## simulate an ESM panel: 30 people, 40 beeps, 3 items set.seed(1) panel <- do.call(rbind, lapply(1:30, function(id) {   y <- matrix(0, 40, 3)   for (t in 2:40) y[t, ] <- c(0.35, 0.30, 0.25) * y[t - 1, ] + rnorm(3)   data.frame(id = id, beep = 1:40, A = y[, 1], B = y[, 2], C = y[, 3]) }))  ## multilevel VAR: temporal, contemporaneous, and between networks fit <- build_mlvar(panel, vars = c("A", "B", "C"), id = "id", beep = "beep")  fit                 # tidy printout of all three networks edges(fit)          # one row per edge (network, from, to, weight) coefs(fit)          # fixed-effect estimates with SE / p / CI plot(fit)           # draw all layers with cograph plot(fit, layer = "temporal")

title: Network Estimation from Intensive Longitudinal Data
code: bayes <- build_mlvar_bayes(panel, vars = c("A", "B", "C"),                            id = "id", beep = "beep",                            n_iter = 4000, n_chains = 2) bayes               # posterior medians, SDs, 95% CIs, convergence (max PSR) coefs(bayes)  ## full DSEM with person-specific slopes, random residuals, and ## within-model imputation of missing observations (needs enough subjects to ## identify the random-effect covariance: at least 2 * (p + p^2) + 1): build_mlvar_bayes(panel, vars = c("A", "B", "C"), id = "id", beep = "beep",                   temporal = "random", residual = "random", impute = TRUE)

title: Network Estimation from Intensive Longitudinal Data
text: Estimators build_var() / build_var_each() — ordinary VAR(1) (OLS), pooled per subject graphical_var() / graphical_var_each() — regularized graphical VAR (GLASSO + EBIC) build_mlvar() — two-step multilevel VAR (temporal, contemporaneous, ) build_mlvar_bayes() — native Bayesian multilevel VAR / DSEM (fixed random slopes, fixed random residual covariance, optional within-model imputation) build_var_bayes() — native Bayesian VAR(1) build_mlvar_mplus() — true-Mplus backend (wraps mlVAR(estimator = "Mplus")) build_usem() — unified Structural Equation Modeling (lavaan) build_gimme() — Group Iterative Multiple Model Estimation Workflow & diagnostics audit_preprocess() — preprocessing audit ILD (compliance, variance, stationarity) estimate_stability() — bootstrap edge-stability diagnostics rolling_var() / rolling_graphical_var() — rolling-window (time-varying) networks validate_forecast() — rolling --sample forecast validation compare_idiographic() — model-comparison reports Tidy accessors (work every result) edges() · nodes() · coefs() · matrices() · summary() · print() · plot() / plot_gimme() · as_netobject()

title: Audit preprocessing and lag construction — audit_preprocess

Name day/session column, NULL. beep Character. Name measurement-occasion column, NULL. scale Logical. Whether standardize variables lagging. center_within Logical. Whether center within person one id present. delete_missings Logical. TRUE, $pairs contains complete current/lagged rows; FALSE, incomplete rows retained NA lags. min_obs Integer NULL. Keep subjects least many observations. subject Optional vector naming subject(s) audit. trend_alpha Numeric p-value cutoff trend flag. ar_threshold Numeric absolute AR(1) cutoff high-persistence flag. mean_shift_threshold Numeric absolute standardized split-half mean shift cutoff. sd_ratio_threshold Numeric split-half SD ratio cutoff. unit_root_t_cutoff Numeric cutoff ADF-style lag-level t-statistic. Values greater cutoff flagged unit-root risk.

what: GIMME: Group Iterative Multiple Model Estimation — build_gimme
title: GIMME: Group Iterative Multiple Model Estimation — build_gimme
text: Estimates person-specific directed networks intensive longitudinal data using unified Structural Equation Modeling (uSEM) framework. Implements data-driven search identifies: Group-level paths: Directed edges present majority (default 75\ Individual-level paths: Additional edges specific person, found group paths established. Uses lavaan SEM estimation modification indices. Accepts single data frame ID column (CSV directories).

title: GIMME: Group Iterative Multiple Model Estimation — build_gimme
code: build_gimme(   data,   vars,   id,   time = NULL,   day = NULL,   beep = NULL,   min_obs = NULL,   subject = NULL,   ar = TRUE,   standardize = FALSE,   groupcutoff = 0.75,   subcutoff = 0.75,   paths = NULL,   exogenous = NULL,   hybrid = FALSE,   VAR = FALSE,   rmsea_cutoff = 0.05,   srmr_cutoff = 0.05,   nnfi_cutoff = 0.95,   cfi_cutoff = 0.95,   n_excellent = 2L,   seed = NULL,   group_correct = "Bonferoni Group",   subgroup = FALSE,   outcome = NULL,   conv_vars = NULL,   mult_vars = NULL,   lv_model = NULL,   lasso_model_crit = NULL,   ms_allow = FALSE,   ordered = NULL,   dir_prop_cutoff = 0,   out = NULL,   sep = NULL,   header = NULL,   plot = FALSE,   sub_feature = "lag & contemp",   sub_method = "Walktrap",   sub_sim_thresh = "lowest",   confirm_subgroup = NULL,   conv_length = 16,   conv_interval = 1,   mean_center_mult = FALSE,   diagnos = FALSE,   ms_tol = 1e-05,   lv_estimator = "miiv",   lv_scores = "regression",   lv_miiv_scaling = "first.indicator",   lv_final_estimator = "miiv" )

title: GIMME: Group Iterative Multiple Model Estimation — build_gimme
text: data data.frame long format columns person ID, time-varying variables, optionally time/beep column. vars Character vector variable names model. id Character string naming person-ID column. time Character string naming time/order column, NULL. provided, data sorted id time lagging. day Character string naming day/session column, NULL. supplied, lag-1 pairs formed within (id, day) block, lag never crosses overnight gap. beep Character string naming measurement-occasion column, NULL. Used (day) order observations time given. min_obs Integer NULL. Keep subjects least many observations (counts taken data). subject Optional vector naming exact subject(s) analyse. ar Logical. TRUE (default), autoregressive paths (variable predicting lag 1) included fixed paths. standardize Logical. TRUE (default FALSE), variables standardized per person estimation. Note: returned coefficient network ($coefs, $psi, $temporal_avg, $contemporaneous_avg, $group_paths) unaffected Nestimate extracts standardized lavaan solution (lavInspect(fit, "std")), invariant input scaling. scale-dependent $fit statistics (chisq, aic, bic) change. groupcutoff Numeric 0 1. Proportion individuals path must significant added group level. Default 0.75. subcutoff Numeric. Subgroup cutoff (default 0.75, matching gimme); relevant subgrouping, implemented. paths Character vector lavaan-syntax paths force model (e.g., "V2~V1lag"). Default NULL. exogenous Character vector variable names treat exogenous. Default NULL. hybrid Logical. TRUE, also searches residual covariances. Default FALSE. VAR Logical. TRUE, fit standard VAR: lagged directed paths searched contemporaneous relations estimated residual covariances (directed contemporaneous paths). Matches gimme(VAR = TRUE). Default FALSE. rmsea_cutoff Numeric. RMSEA threshold excellent fit (default 0.05). srmr_cutoff Numeric. SRMR threshold excellent fit (default 0.05). nnfi_cutoff Numeric. NNFI/TLI threshold excellent fit (default 0.95). cfi_cutoff Numeric. CFI threshold excellent fit (default 0.95). n_excellent Integer. Number fit indices must excellent stop individual search. Default 2. seed Integer NULL. Random seed reproducibility. group_correct Character. Group-level multiple-comparison correction; "Bonferoni Group" (gimme default) implemented. subgroup Logical. Subgrouping (S-GIMME) implemented; TRUE raises error pointing gimme::gimme(). title: GIMME: Group Iterative Multiple Model Estimation — build_gimme
text: S3 object class "net_gimme" containing: temporal p x p matrix group-level temporal (lagged) path counts – entry [,j] = number individuals path j(t-1)->(t). contemporaneous p x p matrix group-level contemporaneous path counts – entry [,j] = number individuals path j(t)->(t). coefs List per-person p x 2p coefficient matrices (rows = endogenous, cols = [lagged, contemporaneous]). psi List per-person 2p x 2p residual covariance matrices c(lag_names, varnames) (standardized lavaan psi block), p x p current-variable block alone. fit Data frame per-person fit indices (chisq, df, pvalue, rmsea, srmr, nnfi, cfi, bic, aic, logl, status). path_counts p x 2p matrix: many individuals path. paths List per-person character vectors lavaan path syntax. group_paths Character vector group-level paths found. individual_paths List per-person character vectors individual-level paths (beyond group). syntax List per-person full lavaan syntax strings. labels Character vector variable names. n_subjects Integer. Number individuals. n_obs Integer vector. Time points per individual. config List configuration parameters.

title: GIMME: Group Iterative Multiple Model Estimation — build_gimme
code: # \donttest{ # Create simple panel data (3 subjects, 4 variables, 50 time points). set.seed(42) n_sub <- 3; n_t <- 50; vars <- paste0("V", 1:4) rows <- lapply(seq_len(n_sub), function(i) {   d <- as.data.frame(matrix(rnorm(n_t * 4), ncol = 4))   names(d) <- vars; d$id <- i; d }) panel <- do.call(rbind, rows) res <- build_gimme(panel, vars = vars, id = "id") print(res) #> GIMME Network Analysis #> ------------------------------  #> Subjects:   3  #> Variables:  4  ( V1, V2, V3, V4 ) #> AR paths:   yes  #> Hybrid:     no  #>  #> Group-level paths found: 0  #>  #> Individual-level paths:  mean 0.0, range 0-0 #>  #> Proportion of subjects with each path: #>  #>   Temporal [directed] #>     weights [1.000, 1.000]  |  +4 / -0 edges #>        V1 V2 V3 V4 #>     V1  1  0  0  0 #>     V2  0  1  0  0 #>     V3  0  0  1  0 #>     V4  0  0  0  1 #>  #>   Contemporaneous [directed] #>     no non-zero edges #>        V1 V2 V3 V4 #>     V1  0  0  0  0 #>     V2  0  0  0  0 #>     V3  0  0  0  0 #>     V4  0  0  0  0 #>  #>   plot(x)  (faithful gimme-style mixed network) | plot(x, layer = "temporal")  #>   edges(x) | nodes(x) | summary(x) | coefs(x) | matrices(x) # }

text: data data.frame containing panel data. vars Character vector variable column names model. id Character string naming person-ID column. day Character string naming day/session column, NULL. provided, lag pairs formed within day. beep Character string naming measurement-occasion column, NULL. NULL, row position within (id, day) used. lags Integer. Lag order; 1 supported (mlVAR's lags). estimator Character. "lmer" / "default" implemented; "lm" / "Mplus" raise error (use mlVAR::mlVAR()). temporal, contemporaneous Character. "fixed" implemented (idiographic clean-room mlVAR's fixed-effects path). random-effects modes ("correlated", "orthogonal", "unique") raise error pointing mlVAR::mlVAR(). AR Logical. TRUE, estimate autoregressive (-lag) temporal effects, giving diagonal temporal matrix (matches mlVAR(AR = TRUE)). Default FALSE. scale Logical. TRUE, variable grand-mean centered divided pooled SD augmentation (mlVAR's scale). Default FALSE. (deprecated standardize alias.) scaleWithin Logical. title: Build a Multilevel Vector Autoregression (mlVAR) network — build_mlvar
text: dual-class c("net_mlvar", "netobject_group") object — named list three full netobjects, one per network, plus model-level metadata stored attributes. element standard c("netobject", "cograph_network") weight-matrix wrapper (raw $data), print(), summary(), coefs(), cograph::splot(fit$temporal) work directly. three constituents matrix-wrapped carry underlying panel data, data-resampling workflow (bootstrap, reliability, stability) must start original panel rather wrappers. Structure: fit$temporal Directed netobject d x d matrix fixed-effect lagged coefficients. $weights[, j] effect variable j t-lag variable t. method = "mlvar_temporal", directed = TRUE. fit$contemporaneous Undirected netobject d x d partial-correlation network within-person lmer residuals. method = "mlvar_contemporaneous", directed = FALSE. fit$Undirected netobject d x d partial-correlation network person means, derived D (- Gamma). method = "mlvar_between", directed = FALSE. Convention: random-intercept SD 0 network estimable; idiographic returns -zero matrix (warning) plotting-oriented convention, whereas mlVAR returns -NA matrix. contemporaneous network follows zero--degeneracy convention. deliberate departure strict reference equivalence singular case. attr(fit, "coefs") / coefs() Tidy data.frame one row per (outcome, predictor) pair columns outcome, predictor, beta, se, t, p, ci_lower, ci_upper, significant. Filter, sort, plot base R tidyverse. Retrieve coefs(fit). attr(fit, "n_obs") Number rows augmented panel na.omit. attr(fit, "n_subjects") Number unique subjects remaining. attr(fit, "lag") Lag order used. attr(fit, "standardize") Logical; whether pre-augmentation standardization applied.

title: Build a Multilevel Vector Autoregression (mlVAR) network — build_mlvar
text: algorithm follows mlVAR's lmer pipeline exactly: Drop rows NA id/day/beep optionally grand-mean standardize variable. Expand per-(id, day) beep grid right-join original values, producing augmented panel (augData). Add within-person lagged predictors (L1_*) person-mean predictors (PM_*). outcome variable fit lmer(y ~ within + -except--PM + (1 | id)) REML = FALSE. Collect fixed-effect temporal matrix B, -effect matrix Gamma, random-intercept SDs (mu_SD), lmer residual SDs. Contemporaneous network: cor2pcor(D %*% cov2cor(cor(resid)) %*% D). -subjects network: cor2pcor(pseudoinverse(forcePositive(D (- Gamma)))). Validated machine precision (max_diff < 1e-10) mlVAR::mlVAR() 25 real ESM datasets openesm 20 simulated configurations (seeds 201-220). See tmp/mlvar_equivalence_real20.R tmp/mlvar_equivalence_20seeds.R.

title: Build a Multilevel Vector Autoregression (mlVAR) network — build_mlvar
code: # \donttest{ set.seed(1) n_id <- 8; n_t <- 30; vars <- c("A", "B", "C") rows <- lapply(seq_len(n_id), function(i) {   m <- as.data.frame(matrix(rnorm(n_t * 3), ncol = 3))   names(m) <- vars   m$id <- i; m$day <- 1L; m$beep <- seq_len(n_t)   m }) d <- do.call(rbind, rows) fit <- build_mlvar(d, vars = vars, id = "id", day = "day", beep = "beep") #> Warning: Model for 'A': singular fit (random-effects variance near zero). #> Warning: Model for 'A': boundary (singular) fit: see help('isSingular') #> Warning: Model for 'B': singular fit (random-effects variance near zero). #> Warning: Model for 'B': boundary (singular) fit: see help('isSingular') #> Warning: Model for 'C': singular fit (random-effects variance near zero). #> Warning: Model for 'C': boundary (singular) fit: see help('isSingular') #> Warning: Between-subjects network not estimable: a random-intercept SD is 0 (no between-person variance). Returning a zero matrix by convention (mlVAR returns NA here). print(fit) #> mlVAR result: 8 subjects, 232 observations, 3 variables (lag 1) #>   Temporal edges significant at p<0.05: 1 / 9 #>  #>   Temporal [directed] #>     weights [-0.131, 0.062]  |  +3 / -6 edges #>           A     B     C #>     A -0.13 -0.05 -0.03 #>     B -0.01 -0.08 -0.01 #>     C  0.06  0.02  0.04 #>  #>   Contemporaneous [undirected] #>     weights [0.006, 0.085]  |  +3 / -0 edges #>          A    B    C #>     A 0.00 0.06 0.08 #>     B 0.06 0.00 0.01 #>     C 0.08 0.01 0.00 #>  #>   Between [undirected] #>     no non-zero edges #>       A B C #>     A 0 0 0 #>     B 0 0 0 #>     C 0 0 0 #>  #>   plot(x) | plot(x, layer = "temporal") | plot(x, layer = "between")  #>   edges(x) | nodes(x) | summary(x) | coefs(x) | matrices(x) summary(fit) #>           network n_nodes n_edges density mean_abs_weight n_positive n_negative #> 1        temporal       3       6       1      0.03140474          2          4 #> 2 contemporaneous       3       3       1      0.05004674          3          0 #> 3         between       3       0       0      0.00000000          0          0 # }

what: Build a Bayesian multilevel VAR network (Mplus DSEM equivalent) — build_mlvar_bayes
title: Build a Bayesian multilevel VAR network (Mplus DSEM equivalent) — build_mlvar_bayes
text: Native, pure-R Bayesian estimator two-level VAR(1) statistically reproduces Mplus DSEM output (estimator behind mlVAR::mlVAR(estimator = "Mplus")) without needing Mplus installed. conjugate Gibbs sampler estimates fixed temporal matrix, within-person residual (contemporaneous) network, -person network, using latent mean centering Mplus's default priors. Point estimates posterior medians posterior SDs 95% credible intervals.

title: Build a Bayesian multilevel VAR network (Mplus DSEM equivalent) — build_mlvar_bayes
code: build_mlvar_bayes(   data,   vars,   id,   day = NULL,   beep = NULL,   lags = 1L,   temporal = c("fixed", "default", "random"),   contemporaneous = c("fixed", "default"),   residual = c("fixed", "random"),   scale = TRUE,   scaleWithin = FALSE,   tinterval = NULL,   impute = FALSE,   n_iter = 4000L,   n_burnin = NULL,   n_chains = 2L,   thin = 1L,   seed = NULL,   min_obs = NULL,   subject = NULL,   verbose = FALSE )

title: Build a Bayesian multilevel VAR network (Mplus DSEM equivalent) — build_mlvar_bayes
text: data data.frame containing panel data. vars Character vector variable column names model (length >= 2). id Character string naming person-ID column. day Character string naming day/session column, NULL. beep Character string naming measurement-occasion column, NULL. NULL, row position within (id, day) block used. lags Integer lag order; 1 supported (matches Mplus DSEM defaults ). temporal Character. "fixed" (default) fits fixed temporal effects random intercepts (Mplus DSEM temporal = "fixed"). "random" fits full DSEM person-specific temporal matrices B_i full random-effect covariance (mu_i, vec(B_i)); temporal network reports posterior mean transition matrix attr(fit, "slope_sd") holds per-coefficient random-slope SDs. "random" needs subjects estimable random-effect covariance: least 2 * (p + p^2) + 1 subjects. contemporaneous Character. "fixed" implemented. residual Character. "fixed" (default) uses one shared population within-person residual covariance. "random" (temporal = "random") gives subject residual covariance Sigma_W_i via conjugate hierarchical inverse-Wishart (Sigma_W_i ~ IW(Lambda, p + 2), Lambda ~ Wishart), matching DSEM person-specific innovation variances; reported contemporaneous network population-average residual covariance. scale Logical. Global grand-mean/SD standardization variable fitting (Mplus/mlVAR scale = TRUE). Default TRUE. scaleWithin Logical. Additionally within-person scale variable. Default FALSE. tinterval Numeric NULL. supplied, beep treated continuous time variable binned onto regular grid width (Mplus TINTERVAL); integer bin becomes occasion index gap-aware lagging, multiple observations one (id, day, bin) slot collapsed first. Lagging gap-aware cases: lag-1 pairs formed consecutive occasions, missing occasions never create spurious lag pairs. Default NULL. impute Logical. TRUE (temporal = "random"), missing observations imputed within model MCMC iteration (data augmentation), rather dropped: person's series expanded full occasion grid every latent cell drawn Gaussian full conditional (outcome t predictor t+1), using vectorised even/odd (checkerboard) block sweep. matches Mplus / Stan / JAGS handle missing data removes listwise-deletion bias heavy missingness, extra computational cost. Default FALSE. n_iter Integer. Total MCMC iterations per chain. Default 4000. n_burnin Integer. Burn-iterations discarded per chain. Default n_iter / 2 (Mplus's first-half burn-convention). n_chains Integer. Number independent chains. Default 2. thin Integer. Keep every thin-th post-burn-draw. Default 1. seed Integer NULL. Base RNG seed (chain c uses seed + c). min_obs Integer NULL. Keep subjects least many observations fitting. subject Optional vector naming exact subject(s) analyse. verbose Logical. Emit progress messages. Default FALSE.

title: Build a Bayesian multilevel VAR network (Mplus DSEM equivalent) — build_mlvar_bayes
text: net_mlvar_bayes object (also inheriting net_mlvar), named list three netobjects (temporal, contemporaneous, ) posterior-summary attributes. coefs() returns tidy table estimate (posterior median), posterior_sd, ci_lower, ci_upper, p (one-tailed), significant (95% CI excludes 0). Posterior draws max Gelman-Rubin PSR kept attributes.

title: Build a Bayesian multilevel VAR network (Mplus DSEM equivalent) — build_mlvar_bayes
text: sampler alternates five conjugate full-conditional draws per iteration: latent person means mu_i (Gaussian), fixed temporal matrix B (matrix-normal), within residual covariance Sigma_W (inverse-Wishart), grand mean alpha (Gaussian), covariance Sigma_B (inverse-Wishart). lagged predictor recentred current mu_i draw every iteration (latent mean centering). Data globally standardized first (matching mlVAR's scale = TRUE); first observation block used lag (condition--first). Validated statistical (Monte-Carlo-error) equivalence real Mplus 9 DSEM output standardized synthetic panels: posterior medians B, Sigma_W, Sigma_B agree Mplus well within posterior SD.

title: Build a Bayesian multilevel VAR network (Mplus DSEM equivalent) — build_mlvar_bayes
code: # \donttest{ set.seed(1) n_id <- 10; n_t <- 40; vars <- c("A", "B") rows <- lapply(seq_len(n_id), function(i) {   y <- matrix(0, n_t, 2)   for (t in 2:n_t) y[t, ] <- c(0.3, 0.15) * y[t - 1, ] + rnorm(2)   data.frame(id = i, beep = seq_len(n_t), A = y[, 1], B = y[, 2]) }) d <- do.call(rbind, rows) fit <- build_mlvar_bayes(d, vars = vars, id = "id", beep = "beep",                          n_iter = 2000, seed = 1) print(fit) #> Bayesian mlVAR (Mplus DSEM equivalent, temporal = fixed): 10 subjects, 390 observations, 2 variables #>   MCMC: 2 chains x 2000 iter (1000 burn-in), 2000 draws | max PSR = 1.003 #>   Temporal 95% CIs excluding 0: 2 / 4 #>  #>   Temporal [directed] #>     weights [-0.083, 0.256]  |  +2 / -2 edges #>           A     B #>     A  0.26 -0.08 #>     B -0.07  0.13 #>  #>   Contemporaneous [undirected] #>     weights [-0.005, -0.005]  |  +0 / -1 edges #>       A B #>     A 0 0 #>     B 0 0 #>  #>   Between [undirected] #>     weights [0.201, 0.201]  |  +1 / -0 edges #>         A   B #>     A 0.0 0.2 #>     B 0.2 0.0 #>  #>   coefs(x) posterior median/SD/CI | matrices(x) | edges(x) | summary(x) coefs(fit) #>   outcome predictor    estimate posterior_sd    ci_lower   ci_upper     p #> 1       A         A  0.25617841   0.05061229  0.15903046 0.35618498 0.000 #> 2       A         B -0.07096325   0.05116161 -0.17225969 0.03004842 0.078 #> 3       B         A -0.08325036   0.05187507 -0.18856429 0.01414300 0.045 #> 4       B         B  0.12607025   0.05115064  0.02490567 0.22468417 0.009 #>   significant #> 1        TRUE #> 2       FALSE #> 3       FALSE #> 4        TRUE # }

title: Graphical VAR Estimation — graphical_var
text: data data.frame matrix columns variables, optionally id, day, beep columns panel/ESM data. vars Character vector variable names. id Character. Name person-ID column. NULL, assumes single subject. day Character. Name day/session column. Default: NULL. beep Character. Name beep/measurement column. Default: NULL. lags Integer. Lag order. 1 supported (matches graphicalVAR's default; multi-lag implemented). Default: 1. n_lambda Integer. Number lambda values per penalty dimension. Default: 50 (matches graphicalVAR's nLambda). gamma Numeric. EBIC hyperparameter (0 = BIC, higher = sparser). Default: 0.5. scale Logical. Whether standardize variables. Default: TRUE. center_within Logical. Whether center within person one id present (removes -person variance). Default: TRUE. lambda_min_ratio Numeric. Ratio min/max lambda applied beta kappa grids unless overridden per-dimension. Default: 0.05. lambda_min_kappa, lambda_min_beta Numeric NULL. Per-dimension min/max lambda ratios (matching graphicalVAR's lambda_min_kappa / lambda_min_beta). NULL, fall back lambda_min_ratio. penalize_diagonal Logical. Penalize autoregressive diagonal beta. Default: TRUE (matches graphicalVAR). lambda_beta Numeric scalar (vector), NULL. supplied, temporal penalty pinned value instead EBIC-selected grid – matching graphicalVAR's lambda_beta argument (e.g. lambda_beta = 0.1). Default NULL (EBIC grid). lambda_kappa Numeric scalar (vector), NULL. lambda_beta contemporaneous (kappa) penalty. regularize_mat_beta Optional numeric/logical matrix (p x p p x (p+1)) per-element beta penalty multipliers (matches graphicalVAR's regularize_mat_beta). NULL uses penalize_diagonal. regularize_mat_kappa Optional p x p numeric/logical matrix per-element kappa penalty multipliers (matches graphicalVAR's regularize_mat_kappa). NULL penalizes -diagonals. maxit_in, maxit_out Integer. Max inner (beta) / outer (beta-kappa) iterations. Defaults 100 (matches maxit./ maxit.). delete_missings Logical. Drop rows missing current/lagged values. Default TRUE (matches deleteMissings). likelihood Either "unpenalized" (default; refit precision EBIC, matching graphicalVAR) "penalized" (use regularized kappa directly). ebic_tol Numeric. Tolerance EBIC tie-break. Default 1e-4. mimic Character. "current" supported (legacy compatibility modes ignored warning). verbose Logical. Emit progress messages. Default FALSE. min_obs Integer NULL. Keep subjects least many observations (counts taken data). Default NULL. subject Optional vector naming exact subject(s) analyse. Default NULL (subjects).

title: Graphical VAR Estimation — graphical_var
text: list class gvar_result containing: beta Temporal coefficient matrix, outcome x (intercept + predictors), graphicalVAR's convention. temporal p x p temporal slice beta[, -1] [outcome, predictor] (intercept dropped). kappa Precision matrix (p x p, symmetric). PCC Partial contemporaneous correlations -cov2cor(kappa), diagonal zeroed. PDC Partial directed correlations. contemporaneous Alias PCC. labels Variable names. n_obs Number valid lag-pair observations. lambda_beta, lambda_kappa Selected penalties. gamma, EBIC EBIC gamma used selected EBIC.

title: Graphical VAR Estimation — graphical_var
text: clean-room reimplementation Rothman/Epskamp two-step estimator numerically equivalent graphicalVAR::graphicalVAR(): identical data preparation (global scaling, optional within-person centering, intercept column, lag-1 construction within id/day blocks), identical lambda grids (generate_lambdas), coupled MRCE beta-update / glasso kappa-update loop, unpenalized-likelihood EBIC, tie-broken model selection. well-conditioned data agrees graphicalVAR machine precision (~1e-11); rank-deficient data (n close number parameters) agrees MRCE inner-solver tolerance (~1e-3), graphicalVAR shares.

title: Graphical VAR Estimation — graphical_var
text: Epskamp, S., Waldorp, L. J., Mottus, R., & Borsboom, D. (2018). Gaussian Graphical Model Cross-Sectional Time-Series Data. Multivariate Behavioral Research, 53(4), 453-480. Rothman, . J., Levina, E., & Zhu, J. (2010). Sparse multivariate regression covariance estimation. JCGS, 19(4), 947-962.

title: Validate one-step forecasts from idiographic VAR models — validate_forecast
code: set.seed(1) d <- data.frame(id = 1, day = rep(1:5, each = 12),                 beep = rep(1:12, 5),                 A = rnorm(60), B = rnorm(60), C = rnorm(60)) fc <- validate_forecast(d, vars = c("A", "B", "C"), id = "id",                         day = "day", beep = "beep",                         initial = 3, n_splits = 2, scale = FALSE) fc$metrics #>   variable  n       mae      rmse       bias #> 1        A 22 0.6627637 0.7916495  0.2286442 #> 2        B 22 0.7710222 0.8981746 -0.1662394 #> 3        C 22 1.0826584 1.3570916  0.8713974 #> 4 .overall 66 0.8388148 1.0448483  0.3112674

title: idiographic (development version)
text: build_mlvar_bayes() — two-level Bayesian VAR(1) latent mean centering. temporal = "fixed" matches Mplus DSEM fixed temporal + random intercepts; temporal = "random" fits full DSEM person-specific temporal matrices random-effect covariance (reports random-slope SDs). build_var_bayes() — single-level Bayesian VAR(1), unregularized Bayesian analogue graphical_var(). Pure-R conjugate Gibbs sampler (hand-rolled inverse-Wishart draws; new dependencies). Posterior median / SD / 95% CI / one-tailed p, three networks (temporal, contemporaneous, ), Gelman-Rubin PSR diagnostic. Validated statistical (Monte-Carlo-error) equivalence real Mplus 9 output; frozen ground-truth fixtures parity tests tests/testthat/fixtures/mplus/. See MPLUS-EQUIVALENCE.md.

title: idiographic 0.1.0
text: Initial CRAN submission. Provides idiographic network estimators intensive longitudinal data, including ordinary VAR, graphical VAR, mlVAR, uSEM, GIMME-style models. Includes preprocessing audits, rolling-window estimation, forecast validation, edge stability diagnostics, model comparison, tidy accessors, cograph plotting support.
